history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the initial Lombok property bump step due to AssertJ test source generation conflicts unrelated to the recipe changes.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the initial property-only step bypasses the immediate compilation failure; subsequent steps will handle plugin and build upgrades, and we will re-introduce the Lombok bump under JDK 21 after the build configuration is updated.

history.qwen_observation: The `upgrade_plugins_for_java21` step failed during the post-recipe build phase because the AssertJ assertion generator produced code referencing inaccessible nested test classes, likely triggered by a plugin version bump or stricter compiler validation.
history.qwen_rationale: Skipping the explicit plugin upgrade step avoids the problematic version change that triggered the AssertJ generator failure. Proceeding directly with `UpgradeBuildToJava21` under JDK 21 will update the compiler and surefire plugins safely, after which we can bump Lombok and apply Java 21 code transforms without hitting the same visibility error.

history.qwen_observation: The build fails immediately due to AssertJ generator producing invalid code for package-private inner classes when compiling under JDK 21, causing method signature conflicts and accessibility errors.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading the AssertJ assertions generator plugin to a recent version (2.3.0) resolves known code generation bugs with inner classes and improves JDK 21 compatibility. Running this under JDK 17 first ensures the plugin upgrade applies cleanly before bumping the Java version, allowing subsequent steps to compile successfully.
